column6: Paka turns productivity into something you'll actually enjoy. Pair your daily to-dos and habits with a hand-drawn alpaca companion that reacts to your real-life progress. Complete tasks to earn rewards, personalize your space, and unlock delightful surprises along the way, all without pressure or feeling overwhelmed. Just a supportive little companion cheering you on, one step at a time.
column13: Paka is built by Tom Yu and Caroline Director. Tom is an Austin-based mobile developer who has shipped apps across just about every category except "productivity" (until now). After losing one too many evenings to game addictions he swore he'd quit, he decided to build the app that would finally keep him on track. Caroline is an LA-based director and storyboard artist with over a decade in the animation industry, responsible for making you emotionally attached to fictional alpacas. Together they're building the character-driven virtual companion app they always wished existed: one where your companions have real personality, real stories, and real opinions about your screen time.

column5: Bodie Malik (developer, he/him) Mason Cooke (musician, he/him)
column6: Take on missions to fight the robot uprising and bring peace to the world. Chose your targets carefully to build combos and manage your heat level in this retro on-rails shooter. Every shot you make counts!

column13: I am Bodie, an Indie Developer! My interest in game development spawned from building custom levels in LittleBigPlanet 2, and every year since then I have challenged myself to learn new skills and create new experiences. I've worked on game jams, personal projects and contracts for other companies for some years now, but I am finally starting to take my own first steps in building and launching my own game from scratch!

column6: Hauntworks is a dark comedy management sim about running a supernatural haunted house in limbo. Your task is to satisfy five otherworldly patrons by harvesting screams, vomit, and laughter from the living. Build your escape, or build a life here.
column13: Elbow Elbow Games is the indie studio of Chris Llewellyn Roberts, a developer based just outside Austin, Texas. After thirteen years in software, four of them leading teams as a manager, he left to build games full-time in July 2025. His work pairs a playful streak with a real fascination for fear, built on the actual craft of haunted-attraction design and the psychology of why we love to be scared.
